title: How AI Is Actually Being Used by Therapists
content:
AI in mental health is often framed as replacing therapists.

But the most practical use of AI today is far simpler: reducing documentation burden.

Therapists spend significant time writing:

- Progress notes
- SOAP notes
- Treatment plans
- Session summaries

AI helps by generating structured documentation based on clinician input.

This keeps therapists in control while improving efficiency.

Common use cases:

- AI therapy notes
- Session summaries
- Documentation templates
- Workflow automation

Learn more:

[AI for therapists](https://aftersession.ai/ai-for-therapists)
[AI therapy notes](https://aftersession.ai/ai-therapy-notes)
[Therapy documentation best practices](https://aftersession.ai/therapy-documentation-best-practices)

AI isn’t replacing therapists.

It’s helping reduce burnout and improve workflows.
